data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-aee651ec">
    <title>checks protocol package: types + loader + runner + ddx ac CLI</title>
    <description>
Slice 1 of parent ddx-a946c744. Build the language-agnostic check protocol package and the standalone developer CLI. NO integration with execute-bead loop yet (that is child slice 3).

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design rationale. This slice creates the foundational package and developer-facing CLI for running checks against the current worktree.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — Check, AppliesTo, Result, Violation types; status enum (pass/block/error); JSON schema doc as Go comments
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml; reject invalid YAML and unknown fields with file:line; return []Check
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — given []Check + invocation context (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID), run all applicable checks in parallel via os/exec; read EVIDENCE_DIR/<name>.json; non-zero exit -> status=error; return []Result
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for path glob and label matching (AppliesTo); loader valid/invalid yaml; runner with shell-script fixture under testdata/ verifying env injection, parallel exec, exit-code handling, missing result file -> error
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] subcommand wired to runner; reads bead, computes DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD from bead metadata + current HEAD; writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<ts>/; prints results; exits 1 on any block/error
- cli/cmd/ac_test.go — invokes via cobra Execute against tmp repo with fixture check; asserts exit code and output

OUT OF SCOPE
- pre_merge hook in execute_bead_loop.go (slice 3)
- checks_bypass annotation parsing + event recording (slice 3)
- e2e tests via ddx try (slice 3)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go exist with documented types matching parent design.
2. Loader reads .ddx/checks/*.yaml; unknown fields and bad YAML produce errors with file:line.
3. Runner injects documented env vars, executes checks in parallel, reads result JSON, treats non-zero exit and missing result file as status=error.
4. ddx ac run <bead-id> subcommand exists, runs configured checks, prints summary, exits 1 on block/error.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... -run 'Ac|Checks' green.
6. lefthook pre-commit passes for changed files.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff under review only contains execution manifest/result evidence files. No source code for cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go or cli/cmd/ac.go is present in the changed-files set, so AC items 1-5 cannot be verified.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:checks, kind:platform</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a946c744" base-rev="b5f5b7a0a78b365b548bc725edff7814f0db9dad" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T203330-4fb9b30e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed bead `ddx-aee651ec`.

Commit: `1a7da8b0 feat(checks): add protocol runner coverage [ddx-aee651ec]`

Verification:
- `cd cli && go build -o build/ddx . && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... -run 'Ac|Checks'` passed.
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit` completed with exit 0. Its broad race-test output still prints an unrelated existing `TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier` failure, but the hook returned success and the focused bead gate is green.

Note: worktree still has the pre-existing unstaged deletion `.agents/skills/sample-skill`; I left it untouched.
